info.title: Power Platform Inventory
info.description: MCP server and typed operations for querying the Power Platform inventory (PowerPlatformResources table in Azure Resource Graph). Exposes every sample query from the Microsoft Learn documentation as a callable operation so admins can count resources, find specific items, analyze connector usage, and run advanced KQL queries from Copilot Studio or Power Automate.

post.description: Model Context Protocol endpoint for Copilot Studio integration. Exposes every inventory sample query as an MCP tool.

get.description: Returns the total number of Power Platform resources in the tenant (KQL: PowerPlatformResources | count).

totalCount.description: Total number of Power Platform resources

get.description: Returns the count of resources grouped by resource type (canvas apps, model-driven apps, cloud flows, agents, etc.).

resourceCount.description: Number of resources of this type

get.description: Returns the count of resources grouped by environment ID.

resourceCount.description: Number of resources in this environment

get.description: Returns the count of resources grouped by Azure region (location field).

resourceCount.description: Number of resources in this region

get.description: Returns the users (owner object IDs) with the most Power Platform resources. Useful for identifying champions and orphan-risk users.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Maximum number of owners to return

get.description: Returns resources created within the last N hours (default 24).

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Maximum number of resources to return

get.description: Finds a single resource by its unique name/ID across the tenant. Returns the full record including all properties.

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: The unique resource ID (the 'name' field — for example, a Copilot Studio agent's GUID).

parameters.name: resourceType
parameters.description: Optional resource type filter (for example, microsoft.copilotstudio/agents). Speeds up the lookup.

properties.description: Full property bag for the resource

get.description: Lists Power Platform resources of a given type with optional environment filter and paging. Use this for tabular inventory views.

parameters.name: resourceType
parameters.description: The resource type to list (for example, microsoft.powerapps/canvasapps).

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Maximum number of resources to return per page.

parameters.name: skipToken
parameters.description: Continuation token from a previous response.

skipToken.description: Continuation token for next page (empty when finished)

get.description: Lists all environments in the inventory with type, state, group assignment, and region.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Maximum number of environments to return

get.description: Lists all environment groups in the tenant.

Lists the connectors used by the most distinct resources across canvas apps, model-driven apps, cloud flows, agent flows, workflow agent flows, and Copilot Studio agents. (Connector inventory preview.)

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Maximum number of connectors to return

rows.description: Connectors ranked by distinct resource count

resourceCount.description: Number of distinct resources using this connector

get.description: Shows how many resources use 0, 1, 2, or more connectors. Useful for spotting complexity outliers. (Connector inventory preview.)

connectorCount.description: Number of connectors used by a resource

resourceCount.description: Number of resources with this connector count

get.description: Finds every Power Platform resource that uses a specific connector. Critical for impact analysis when a connector has a known issue, is being deprecated, or requires new licensing. (Connector inventory preview.)

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Maximum number of resources to return

get.description: Lists every connector used in every environment with the count of distinct resources that use it. Useful for adoption analysis and DLP policy decisions. (Connector inventory preview.)

post.description: Advanced escape hatch. Submits a raw Power Platform inventory query (TableName + Clauses + Options) directly to the resource query endpoint. Use when no preset operation fits.

Clauses.description: Array of query clause objects ($type: where, project, take, orderby, distinct, count, summarize, extend, join).

get.description: Internal operation for dynamic dropdowns. Returns environment IDs and display names.
Internal operation for dynamic dropdowns. Returns the static list of supported resource types.
Internal operation for dynamic dropdowns. Returns the distinct connectors observed in the tenant inventory.
